resources.string: Tap here to let Raccoon scan your storage
Raccoon is rummaging through your files\u2026
Oops! %s \u2014 check permissions and give it another go.
Raccoon hit a snag! Check storage permissions and try scanning again.
Files will be moved to trash. You can undo within %1$d seconds.

plurals.name: confirm_delete_detail
plurals.item: %1$d file (%2$s) will be moved to trash.\nYou can undo within %3$d seconds.
%1$d files (%2$s) will be moved to trash.\nYou can undo within %3$d seconds.

resources.string: Raccoon needs permission to scan your files. Your data stays on-device \u2014 nothing is uploaded.\n\nPlease grant \'Allow access to manage all files\' on the next screen.
Storage permission is needed to scan files. Please grant it in Settings so Raccoon can help manage your storage.
Raccoon hasn\'t sniffed out any files yet.\nHead to the Manager tab to start a scan!
Nothing in the den yet!\nLet Raccoon scan your storage first.
Let Raccoon rummage through your storage to see what\'s here
Raccoon needs to scout your storage first!\nHead to the Manager tab and tap Scan.
Raccoon peeked everywhere but found nothing.\nTry a different category or clear the search.
Raccoon searched high and low \u2014 no results for \"%s\"
Raccoon needs scan data to build the tree!\nHead to the Manager tab and tap Scan.
The tree is bare \u2014 no files to display here.
Raccoon is ready to sniff out junk!\nHead to the Manager tab and tap Scan.
Squeaky clean! Raccoon found zero junk. Your storage is spotless.
Raccoon wants to hunt for space hogs!\nHead to the Manager tab and tap Scan.
No big files lurking around \u2014 Raccoon approves of your tidy storage!
Raccoon is eager to catch copycats!\nHead to the Manager tab and tap Scan.
No duplicates spotted! Every file is one-of-a-kind.
No junk in sight \u2014 Raccoon is impressed!
No space hogs here \u2014 your storage is lean!
Zero copycats found \u2014 every file is unique!
Raccoon is waiting to dig in!
Scan your storage and Raccoon will crunch the numbers for you.
No app found to open this file
%s copied \u2014 long-press a file to paste into its folder
%s cut \u2014 long-press a file to paste into its folder
No conversions available for this file type
Re-encode this image as a %1$s file (.%2$s). The original file is kept.
Embed this image into a single-page PDF document.
Scale this image down to fit within a maximum width and height, keeping the aspect ratio.
Enter the maximum dimensions. The image will be scaled down proportionally to fit, preserving its aspect ratio. Images smaller than the specified size will not be enlarged.
The output format will match the original file.
Render each page of this PDF as a separate %s image file at 2x resolution.
Use the slider to pick a point in the video, then save that frame as an image.
Capture evenly-spaced frames across the entire video and save them as individual image files in a new folder.
Save the embedded cover image from this audio file.
Render this text file as a monospace PDF document (A4 pages).
Render this HTML file as a PDF document.
Render this Markdown file as a plain-text PDF document.
Convert CSV data into a neatly aligned text table with column headers.
A file with that name already exists
Raccoon moved %s to its new home!
Raccoon couldn\'t move that file \u2014 check if the destination is accessible.
Raccoon couldn\'t rename that file. Try a different name.
Compression failed: %s. Raccoon will try harder next time.
Only ZIP archives can be extracted
This archive has too many files to extract safely (limit: %d).
%1$d deleted, %2$d failed \u2014 some files may be in use
Raccoon found %1$d files (%2$s) \u2014 %3$d duplicates, %4$d junk, %5$d large
Raccoon is your friendly storage sidekick! It sniffs out duplicates, hunts down large files, and sweeps away junk.\n\nEverything stays on your device \u2014 your data never leaves.

Use the bottom tabs to explore:

• Browse — all your files by category
• Duplicates — files with identical content
• Manager — Raccoon's control center
• Large Files — the biggest space hogs
• Junk Files — caches, temp files, old downloads
Raccoon hasn\'t scanned yet \u2014 run a scan to see the breakdown!
Raccoon is working on it\u2026 Results will appear when complete.
Tap the Manager tab for quick actions: Scan, Analysis, Quick Clean, Tree View, and Janitor.\n\nRaccoon\'s den \u2014 your one-stop control center for tidy storage.
Raccoon\'s den \u2014 your tidy-up command center
Let Raccoon sniff out duplicates, junk, and large files
See where your space goes with a full breakdown
One-tap sweep of junk files and caches
Explore your files in an interactive tree map
Deep clean \u2014 Raccoon goes through everything
Sparkling clean! Nothing to sweep up.
Deep clean finds duplicate files, oversized downloads, and old caches across your entire storage. Review and select what to remove.
Starting deep clean\u2026 Review results in the Duplicates, Large, and Junk tabs.
Raccoon needs a scan first \u2014 tap Scan Storage to get started!
%s in junk files can be freed
Raccoon is done rummaging! Found %1$d files using %2$s.
Raccoon swept away %s \u2014 nice and tidy!
Raccoon is ready to tidy up! Tap Scan to get started.
Let Raccoon scan your storage to see a summary here.
Reorganize files into tidy folders by type and date
Raccoon checked every corner \u2014 your files are already perfectly organized!

plurals.name: optimize_confirm_message
plurals.item: Move %d file to its suggested location?
Move %d files to their suggested locations?

plurals.name: optimize_applied
plurals.item: Raccoon tidied up %d file \u2014 looking organized!
Raccoon tidied up %d files \u2014 looking organized!

resources.string: Pick some suggestions first \u2014 Raccoon needs something to work on!

This tool analyzes your files and suggests moving them into organized folders by type and date. Photos go to Pictures/YYYY-MM, videos to Videos/YYYY-MM, audio to Music, documents to Documents, and old downloads to OldDownloads. Uncheck any files you want to keep in place.
Side-by-side file manager for easy copy and move
No files selected (directories cannot be deleted here)
What would you like to do with %d item(s)?
Browse and transfer files on Google Drive, SFTP, WebDAV
Remove connection \"%s\"? This cannot be undone.
Raccoon hasn\'t ventured to the cloud yet.\nAdd a connection to explore remote files!
Nothing in this folder \u2014 it\'s totally empty.
Raccoon couldn\'t reach %s. Check your settings and try again.
No internet connection \u2014 Raccoon can\'t reach the cloud right now.
Authentication failed \u2014 double-check your credentials and try again.
Connection timed out \u2014 the server may be busy. Try again in a moment.
Server not found \u2014 double-check the hostname and try again.
Upload failed: %s. Check your connection and try again.
Connect to any SSH/SFTP server. Use your server\'s IP address or hostname.
Works with Nextcloud, ownCloud, and other WebDAV servers. Enter the full WebDAV URL.
Paste your Google Drive OAuth2 token. You can generate one from the Google API Console.
Select where you want to connect
Access your Google Drive files, photos, and shared folders
Connect to any SSH/SFTP server on your network or the internet
Compatible with Nextcloud, ownCloud, and other WebDAV services
Scan for malware, root, privacy, and network risks
Raccoon is standing guard. Tap below to check for threats.
All clear! Raccoon gave your device a clean bill of health.
Checking root, hooks, debugger, emulator, dev settings, overlays\u2026
Scanning files for malware hashes, ELF binaries, scripts, patterns\u2026
Auditing permissions, notification listeners, usage tracking\u2026
Checking cleartext traffic, data exfiltration risks, open ports\u2026
Verifying app installers, certificates, signing, sideloads\u2026

This will attempt to resolve all actionable threats:

%s

Proceed?
Raccoon dealt with %d threats \u2014 your device is safer now!
You can leave this screen. The scan will continue and notify you when complete.
No scan history yet \u2014 run your first scan to start tracking.
Remove scan cache, cloud connections, preferences, and scan history
This will delete all scan data, cloud connection credentials, scan history, and reset preferences to defaults.\n\nThis cannot be undone.
Automatically create GitHub Issues when the app crashes. Crash logs are saved to disk and uploaded on the next launch. Requires a GitHub personal access token with repo scope.
%d pending crash report(s) will be sent on next launch

Raccoon File Manager processes your data locally on your device:

• File scanning indexes file names, sizes, and paths to find duplicates, junk, and large files. This data is cached locally and auto-deleted after 30 days.

• Security scanning checks installed apps and their permissions to detect threats. No app data leaves your device.

• Cloud connections store your credentials securely using Android encrypted storage. Credentials are cleared from memory after connecting.

• No data is ever sent to external servers, analytics services, or third parties.

You can clear all stored data at any time from Settings.
File \"%1$s\" matches a known malware filename pattern.
APK file found in %1$s. APKs outside standard install locations may be side-loaded malware.
Linux executable binary \"%1$s\" found in user storage. This could be a hacking tool, exploit, or backdoor.
Android bytecode file \"%1$s\" found outside app directories. This may be a dynamically loaded malware payload.
Script \"%1$s\" contains suspicious commands that could compromise device security (pattern: %2$s).
Hidden file \"%1$s\" (.%2$s) found at %3$s. Malware often uses hidden files to evade detection.
APK \"%1$s\" is %2$dMB. Unusually large APKs may contain packed malware or stolen data.
Archive \"%1$s\" is only %2$d bytes, below the minimum valid size for .%3$s format. This file is likely corrupt.
\"%1$s\" targets Android %2$d and may allow unencrypted HTTP traffic, exposing data to interception.
\"%1$s\" explicitly enables cleartext HTTP traffic in its manifest.
\"%1$s\" has internet access combined with %2$d sensitive permissions (%3$s). This combination could enable data exfiltration.
\"%1$s\" (%2$s) can intercept and inspect network traffic. This could be used to capture passwords, tokens, and private data.
\"%1$s\" (%2$s) is a network penetration/scanning tool. Its presence may indicate unauthorized network activity.
Port %1$d (%2$s) is listening on this device. This is unusual for a mobile device and may indicate a backdoor or misconfigured service.
Android Debug Bridge is accessible over the network on port %1$s. Anyone on the same network can gain full device control.
\"%1$s\" can read/send SMS. This is a common malware capability used for premium SMS fraud and 2FA interception.
\"%1$s\" can track your location continuously, even when the app is not in use.
\"%1$s\" can read your call history, including who you called, when, and for how long.
\"%1$s\" has access to both camera and microphone. This combination enables secret audio/video recording.
\"%1$s\" can install other applications. Malware uses this capability to download and install additional malicious apps (\"dropper\" behavior).
\"%1$s\" can read body sensors and send data over the network. Sensitive health data could be exfiltrated.
\"%1$s\" has permission to read and modify all files on the device, including photos, documents, and app data.
\"%1$s\" can read ALL your notifications, including messages, emails, 2FA codes, and banking alerts.
\"%1$s\" can monitor which apps you use, when, and for how long. This is a significant privacy concern.
\"%1$s\" can see all installed apps on your device. This data can be used for fingerprinting and targeted advertising.
Toggle all folders expanded or collapsed
Raccoon needs scan data to suggest optimizations.\nHead to the Manager tab and tap Scan Storage.
Uses secure OAuth 2.0 \u2014 you\'ll be redirected to sign in
Access your GitHub repositories and gists
Create a personal access token at github.com/settings/tokens and paste it above.
Select all items in this category
